courses.name: Introduction to Cybersecurity
courses.description: to Cybersecurity at La Trobe University | Open Universities Australia Introduction to Cybersecurity Search for courses and subjects Introduction to Cybersecurity Explore the basic principles of cybersecurity. Get familiar with industry terminology, software, and best practices. You’ll learn about the importance of protecting data. Study real-world cyber threats and delve into current solutions. Enrol today with instant approval and no entry requirements No ATAR needed, No prior study Learn more about Pricing Info Learn more about Upfront Cost HECS-HELP and FEE-HELP available Learn more about financial options Introduction to Cybersecurity Need expert guidance? Book a one-on-one call with your own dedicated student advisor for prompt, convenient, unbiased guidance – at no cost. Choose a time that suits you, across 7 days a week Connect with your experienced guide, who'll stay with you throughout study Get the clarity you need to truly unlock your uni options We're not a uni, but we'll help you choose one. Need help now? Call us on
learning_outcomes: On successful completion you will be able to:
Analyse emerging cybersecurity practices, regulations, and standards, and evaluate their implications for digital security.
Demonstrate foundation skills in identifying malware, securing applications, systems, and networks.
Apply and compare approaches to cyber risk management in addressing real-world digital security challenges.
Work efficiently and effectively as a member of a team to investigate possible solutions such as cryptography to enhance data security, and web security, and digital forensics against cyber threats.
courses.assessment: 100% online 5 July 2026 Entry requirements No ATAR needed, No prior study Start dates 13 July 2026 Learn more about Pricing Info Upfront cost Learn more about Upfront Cost Loan available HECS-HELP and FEE-HELP available Learn more about financial options Introduction to Cybersecurity Start studying Need expert guidance? Book a one-on-one call with your own dedicated student advisor for prompt, convenient, unbiased guidance – at no cost. Book a consultation Need help now? Call us on About this subject What you'll learn On successful completion you will be able to: Analyse emerging cybersecurity practices, regulations, and standards, and evaluate their implications for digital security. Demonstrate foundation skills in identifying malware, securing applications, systems, and networks. Apply and compare approaches to cyber risk management in addressing real-world digital security challenges. Work efficiently and effectively as a member of a team to investigate possible solutions such as cryptography to enhance data security, and web security, and digital forensics against cyber threats. Topics covered History, Fundamental Concepts and Terminology of Cybersecurity Software and Application Security History and Evolution of Computer Networks and Threats Basics of Cryptography Safe Handling of Data in Digital Business Description In the modern Internet era, industries and organisations need to be prepared to defend against cyber threats and attacks. Stakeholders should be familiar with basic principles and best practices of cybersecurity to protect their businesses and personal information. In this subject, these principles and strategies for future cyber security are explored. Key topics include: information security; ethical and legal practices; mitigating cyber vulnerabilities; and the process of incident response and analysis. The subject introduces the broad discipline of cybersecurity and outlines how to ensure the privacy, reliability, confidentiality and integrity of information systems and mitigate against cyber threats and risks. Assessments Malware Analysis (equivalent to 750 Words) (25%) Case Study on the world’s biggest data breach (equivalent to 1000 words per student) (35%) Project-based Assessment: Risk Assessment  (equivalent to 1500 Words) (30%) Quiz (equivalent to 500 words) (10%)

courses.name: Cybersecurity in Practice
courses.description: Cybersecurity in Practice online course with La Trobe University, through Open Universities Australia. Study Technology and Information online.
learning_outcomes: On successful completion you will be able to:
Investigate, evaluate and apply current cybersecurity technologies that protect against cyber threats.
Describe the process of threat and vulnerability identification and incident response in cybersecurity environments.
Employ secure software design practices when working in teams.
courses.assessment: 100% online 5 July 2026 Entry requirements Prior study needed Start dates 13 July 2026 Learn more about Pricing Info Upfront cost Learn more about Upfront Cost Loan available HECS-HELP and FEE-HELP available Learn more about financial options Cybersecurity in Practice Start studying Need expert guidance? Book a one-on-one call with your own dedicated student advisor for prompt, convenient, unbiased guidance – at no cost. Choose a time that suits you, across 7 days a week Connect with your experienced guide, who'll stay with you throughout study Get the clarity you need to truly unlock your uni options We're not a uni, but we'll help you choose one. Book a consultation Need help now? Call us on About this subject What you'll learn On successful completion you will be able to: Investigate, evaluate and apply current cybersecurity technologies that protect against cyber threats. Describe the process of threat and vulnerability identification and incident response in cybersecurity environments. Employ secure software design practices when working in teams. Topics covered Computer Security Concepts and OSI Security Architecture. Structure and Function of a Cybersecurity Organisation or Department. Information Classification Based on Sensitivity. Security Frameworks and Standards' Bodies (NIST, ASD, ISO, ENISA, OWASP, ISF). Security Threats and Control Themes. Network Zoning Based on Trust. Look at the Structure of Network Traffic and the Function of the Networking Layers. Learn Some Important Basic Networking Protocols. Network Traffic Control Devices, Such as Firewalls and Intrusion Detection/Prevention. Identification Concepts. Real-World Authentication. Internet identity and federation. The art and science of passwords. The Cyber Kill Chain. Security incident response. Human vulnerabilities. The challenges of security operations. Common software flaws; Sandboxing; OWASP top 10 web vulnerabilities. Description Every organisation and business that intends to protect itself from threats of cyber-attacks needs to know current practices in cyber defence. This subject will introduce common technologies used to identify and mitigate threats including firewalls, intrusion detection systems, intrusion protection systems and honeypots. This subject also introduces incident response processes and simple ways to incorporate secure programming practices to make computer programs less vulnerable to attacks. Assessments Passwords as an identity validator (equivalent to 750 words) (20%) Case Study - Responding to a data breach on the internet (equiv to 1500 words per student) (35%) Project-based Assessment: Software vunerabilities (equiv to 1500 words per student) (35%) Quiz (equivalent to 500 words) (10%)

courses.name: Cryptography and Security
courses.description: Cryptography and Security online course with La Trobe University, through Open Universities Australia. Study Technology and Information online.
learning_outcomes: On successful completion you will be able to:
Explain basic mathematical principles and constructions underlying cryptographic methods and employ these to solve simple problems.
Identify and summarise a range of cryptographic techniques, including private key systems such as AES (Advanced Encryption System), and public key systems such as RSA (Rivest, Shamir, Adleman).
Identify and describe different contexts of application of cryptographic techniques in communication and cybersecurity, such as authentication and secure transmission.
courses.assessment: Subject may require attendance 5 July 2026 Entry requirements No ATAR needed, No prior study Start dates 13 July 2026 Learn more about Pricing Info Upfront cost Learn more about Upfront Cost Loan available HECS-HELP and FEE-HELP available Learn more about financial options Cryptography and Security Start studying Need expert guidance? Choose a time that suits you, across 7 days a week Connect with your experienced guide, who'll stay with you throughout study Get the clarity you need to truly unlock your uni options We're not a uni, but we'll help you choose one. Book a consultation Need help now? Call us on About this subject What you'll learn On successful completion you will be able to: Explain basic mathematical principles and constructions underlying cryptographic methods and employ these to solve simple problems. Identify and summarise a range of cryptographic techniques, including private key systems such as AES (Advanced Encryption System), and public key systems such as RSA (Rivest, Shamir, Adleman). Identify and describe different contexts of application of cryptographic techniques in communication and cybersecurity, such as authentication and secure transmission. Topics covered Introductory mathematics for cryptography Growth and algorithms Introduction to cryptographic systems Pseudorandomness and stream ciphers Public key cryptography Asymmetric cryptography and block ciphers Introduction to elliptic curve cryptography Fundamentals and applications of probability Fundamentals and applications of information theory One way functions and hash functions Digital currency and blockchain Quantum computing and cryptography Module learning with errors taster A taster on error correcting (linear) codes Description This subject provides a strong foundation for cryptography and coding, which are fundamental for mastering cybersecurity. Technical skills in mathematics will be developed, enabling the introduction of a range of common cryptographic techniques, including public and private key encryption, hashing, and cryptanalysis. Assessments Preprogrammed computer generated tests with automated feedback will lead to written problem solving questions.  The online test elements are enabled for early feedback, from start of semester.  Equivalent to 1000 words. (25%) Computer generated test elements will be supplemented by written problem solving, and elements involving computer explorations.  Equivalent to 1000 words. (25%) Computer generated problem solving tests, written problem solving and computational explorations.  Equivalent to 1000 words. (25%) 1 hour Invigilated online exam  Equivalent to 1000 words. (25%)

courses.name: Inside Information Technology
courses.description: Inside Information Technology online course with La Trobe University, through Open Universities Australia. Study Technology and Information online.
learning_outcomes: On successful completion you will be able to:
Demonstrate how a computer system processes and delivers data/information to users.

Interpret how the role of each of the following components of Information Technology (IT) - computer system, computer network, and World Wide Web - supports the goal of delivering data and information to users from around the world.
Apply the Web Development principles to constructing a Web system.
Practise the IT knowledge such as HTML, computer system/network to address typical real-life issues.
courses.assessment: 100% online 5 July 2026 Entry requirements Prior study needed Start dates 13 July 2026 Learn more about Pricing Info Upfront cost Learn more about Upfront Cost Loan available HECS-HELP and FEE-HELP available Learn more about financial options Inside Information Technology Start studying Need expert guidance? Book a one-on-one call with your own dedicated student advisor for prompt, convenient, unbiased guidance – at no cost. Choose a time that suits you, across 7 days a week Connect with your experienced guide, who'll stay with you throughout study Get the clarity you need to truly unlock your uni options We're not a uni, but we'll help you choose one. Book a consultation Need help now? Call us on About this subject What you'll learn On successful completion you will be able to: Demonstrate how a computer system processes and delivers data/information to users. Interpret how the role of each of the following components of Information Technology (IT) - computer system, computer network, and World Wide Web - supports the goal of delivering data and information to users from around the world. Apply the Web Development principles to constructing a Web system. Practise the IT knowledge such as HTML, computer system/network to address typical real-life issues. Topics covered Computer Hardware Components (CPU, Input/Output and Storage Devices, etc) How a Computer Works Computer Networks and it's Different Types World Wide Web System and the Development of a Web System Introduction to Data Base Systems Description Students will be introduced to the rapidly expanding and influential field of information technology which comprises various types of technologies in computers (both hardware and software) and computer networks. It is therefore relevant to students in a wide range of disciplines: for examples, health sciences, business, engineering and technology. Students will be taught the fundamental principles of computer operations, different types of input/output and storage devices, computer networks including Local and Wide area networks, information systems, Database, the Internet, the World Wide Web and HTML. Students will acquire the knowledge and practical skills in information technology and be able to apply them to their field of study. Assessments 11 weekly practice exercises equivalent to 1500 words in total. (30%) Early-term test equivalent to 500 words at the end of Week 4 (15%) Web system development Assignment equivalent to 1500 words (30%) End-of-term test equivalent to 1000 words at the end of Week 11 (25%)

courses.name: Wireless Network and Security
courses.description: Enrolments for this course are closed, but you may have other options to start studying now. Book a consultation to learn more.
learning_outcomes: On successful completion you will be able to:
Apply techniques for addressing the impairments in wireless communication.
Create security risk assessment reports based on the identification and appraisal of various threats and attacks on wireless systems.
Apply suitable wireless network security architectures and settings for homes, enterprises and public environments.
Assess and evaluate potential and existing security risk levels and vulnerabilities in wireless systems.
courses.assessment: Subject may require attendance Entry requirements No ATAR needed, No prior study Start dates Loan available HECS-HELP and FEE-HELP available Learn more about financial options Wireless Network and Security Book a consultation Need expert guidance? Book a one-on-one call with your own dedicated student advisor for prompt, convenient, unbiased guidance – at no cost. Choose a time that suits you, across 7 days a week Connect with your experienced guide, who'll stay with you throughout study Get the clarity you need to truly unlock your uni options We're not a uni, but we'll help you choose one. Book a consultation Need help now? Call us on About this subject What you'll learn On successful completion you will be able to: Apply techniques for addressing the impairments in wireless communication. Create security risk assessment reports based on the identification and appraisal of various threats and attacks on wireless systems. Apply suitable wireless network security architectures and settings for homes, enterprises and public environments. Assess and evaluate potential and existing security risk levels and vulnerabilities in wireless systems. Topics covered Wireless transmission fundamentals Spread spectrum techniques in wireless communications and applications Fundamental concepts of security services (confidentiality, integrity and availability) Cryptography fundamentals including public and private key encryption techniques Wireless local area networks (WLAN) 802.11 security basics including Wired Equivalent Privacy (WEP), Wi-Fi Protected Access (WPA) Description Due to the ubiquitous nature of mobile and wireless technology in our daily lives and business operations, ensuring wireless security is becoming a top priority. This subject explores the security vulnerabilities in wireless networks and covers the fundamental concerns of confidentiality, integrity and availability. The following key areas will be covered: Wireless LAN Security - Wired Equivalent Privacy (WEP), Wi-Fi Protected Access (WPA), Authentication Servers (RADIUS), 802.11i security standard; Securing Ad-Hoc Networks - MAC layer misbehaviour, secure routing protocols, types of attacks and solutions, key management, broadcast authentication. Assessments Problem solving and content knowledge test (or quiz) (equivalent to 1000 words) (25%) Content knowledge and system analysis and design presentation - short report 10% (700 words), - group presentation 15% (300 words per student) (25%) Problem solving, content knowledge, system design exam (equivalent to 2000 words) (50%)

courses.name: Data Security and Information Assurance
courses.description: Data Security and Information Assurance online course with La Trobe University, through Open Universities Australia. Study Technology and Information online.
learning_outcomes: On successful completion you will be able to:
Describe key concepts of data security and information assurance, including the CIA triad, information assets, and common organisational security challenges.
Identify and analyse threats, vulnerabilities, and risks affecting organisational information systems.
Analyse security risks and apply appropriate technical and security controls to protect organisational information systems.
Evaluate security policies, ethical practices, and data maintenance methods to ensure compliance, confidentiality, integrity, and availability of organizational information.

Call us on About this subject What you'll learn On successful completion you will be able to: Describe key concepts of data security and information assurance, including the CIA triad, information assets, and common organisational security challenges. Identify and analyse threats, vulnerabilities, and risks affecting organisational information systems. Analyse security risks and apply appropriate technical and security controls to protect organisational information systems. Evaluate security policies, ethical practices, and data maintenance methods to ensure compliance, confidentiality, integrity, and availability of organizational information. Topics covered Overview of information assurance The fundamental pillars of information security Fundamental concepts of data protection Overview of mathematics used in data protection Assuring data/information confidentiality with reversible cryptography Assuring integrity with one-way cryptography Assuring authenticity with digital signatures Assuring identity with protocols and tokens Assurance in the public domain: blockchain Description This subject will cover basic aspects of information and data security including integrity models, message authentication, authentication tools, access controls and cryptography. Topics will focus on data assurance and prevention methods including data integrity methods. This subject also covers basic cyber-attack defences such as firewalls. Assessments A timed quiz (equivalent to 500 words) completed individually under controlled conditions during the scheduled lab session in Week 4 (10%) Written report on security threats and controls (equivalent to 1000 words) (20%) Group written submission on a Cybersecurity case study on information security (equivalent to 1000 words) (20%) One 2-hour final exam (equiv to 2000 words) (50%)

courses.name: Human Factors in Cybersecurity
courses.description: Human Factors in Cybersecurity online course with La Trobe University, through Open Universities Australia. Study Technology and Information online.
learning_outcomes: On successful completion you will be able to:
Analyse psychological factors influencing cybersecurity behaviours and personality characteristics to develop strategies for secure practices and organisational sustainability goals.

Investigate social implications of human factors in cyber security, considering digital divides, socio-cultural norms, and digital empowerment, to inform policies promoting equitable access and participation in secure environments.

Analyse cybercrime business models and threats impacting corporate social and ethical responsibilities, assessing the influence of organisational culture, leadership, and communication on cyber security practices.
Assess the economic impact of and financial implications of investing in human factors in cyber security and propose cost-effective risk management strategies.

Develop a comprehensive cyber security culture, education and engagement strategy within an organisational context, whilst employing professional skills in evaluative judgement, team building, negotiation, and high-level communication across diverse environments.
courses.assessment: 100% online 5 July 2026 Entry requirements Prior study needed Start dates 13 July 2026 Learn more about Pricing Info Upfront cost Learn more about Upfront Cost Loan available HECS-HELP and FEE-HELP available Learn more about financial options Human Factors in Cybersecurity Start studying Need expert guidance? Choose a time that suits you, across 7 days a week Connect with your experienced guide, who'll stay with you throughout study Get the clarity you need to truly unlock your uni options We're not a uni, but we'll help you choose one. Book a consultation Need help now? Call us on About this subject What you'll learn On successful completion you will be able to: Analyse psychological factors influencing cybersecurity behaviours and personality characteristics to develop strategies for secure practices and organisational sustainability goals. Investigate social implications of human factors in cyber security, considering digital divides, socio-cultural norms, and digital empowerment, to inform policies promoting equitable access and participation in secure environments. Analyse cybercrime business models and threats impacting corporate social and ethical responsibilities, assessing the influence of organisational culture, leadership, and communication on cyber security practices. Assess the economic impact of and financial implications of investing in human factors in cyber security and propose cost-effective risk management strategies. Develop a comprehensive cyber security culture, education and engagement strategy within an organisational context, whilst employing professional skills in evaluative judgement, team building, negotiation, and high-level communication across diverse environments. Topics covered Psychology, sociology and anthropology of human behaviour Vulnerability analysis, social engineering and hacker dynamics Cybercrime financial and economic modelling Ethics, policy and regulation Cybersecurity culture, education and awareness Description This subject examines cybersecurity through a human factors lens across societal, industry, and organisational contexts. Students explore how ethical, social, and psychological factors shape human behaviour and cyber risk, and how these vulnerabilities arise and are exploited. They analyse cybercrime business models and global threats, with a focus on how human factors contribute to attack success and organisational risk. Building on this, students apply human factors principles to design organisational cybersecurity solutions, including cultural, communication, and policy strategies aligned to relevant frameworks. Through this progression, students develop professional skills in evaluative judgement, teamwork, and communication to deliver practical, human-centred cybersecurity solutions. Assessments Society: Community Cyber Campaign  1.Recorded presentation (2minutes, 50%)   2. Infographic (500 word equiv, 50%) (20%) Industry Analysis: Cyber Threats and Human Exploitation  1. Recorded presentation (2minutes, 40%)  2. Digital Industry Communication Artefact (700 word equiv, 60%) (30%) Organisational (Design Solutions): Cyber Clinic Portfolio   Part A: Cyber Clinic Portfolio Artefacts (1500 word equiv, 50%)   Part B: Group Interactive Oral (15mins, 30%)   Part C: Individual Reflection and Peer Evaluation (500word equiv, 20%) (50%)

courses.name: Capstone Project
courses.description: Capstone Project online course with La Trobe University, through Open Universities Australia. Study Business and Management online.
learning_outcomes: On successful completion you will be able to:
Apply advanced project management skills to the implementation phases of a substantive development project.
Devise and implement industry standard technical solutions in software or cybersecurity practices.
Report project development outcomes to technical and non-technical audiences and reflect on feedback.
Construct professional system documentation and advanced technical reports to meet the requirements of software or cybersecurity industry standards.

Construct professional system documentation and advanced technical reports to meet the requirements of software or cybersecurity industry standards. Topics covered ICT Project Implementation ICT Project Deployment ICT Project Testing ICT Project Delivery Description This subject enables you to develop your skills to implement a small or medium-sized project in a team. You will address a real-life problem in software engineering or cybersecurity. The subject covers the skills to manage, implement and report on the delivery of a substantive project to appropriate stakeholders. You will make milestone presentations with your team. You MUST complete both CSE3PM and CSE3CAP in two consecutive semesters. Assessments 5 written Sprint Project management reports (1500 words total) (35%) Project Milestone Presentation (5 minutes/member – 500 words equivalent) - presentation will be conducted by end of week 4 (10%) Final written Project Report (1500 words per student). (35%) Final Project Presentation (1000 word equivalent) (20%)

courses.name: Professional Practices and Entrepreneurship in Information Technology
courses.description: Professional Practices and Entrepreneurship in Information Technology online course with La Trobe University, through Open Universities Australia. Study Technology and Information online.
learning_outcomes: On successful completion you will be able to:
Evaluate diverse funding options for supporting entrepreneurial endeavors while considering the essential development steps.
Critically analyse IT business ventures based on social, legal and ethical issues and examine the responsibilities of IT professionals to employers, clients, and society as part of a team.

Reflect on personal viewpoints related to managing relationships and individual entrepreneurial capacity, effectively developing and presenting personal and professional reflections to become career-ready.
Apply ethical theory to analyse ethical dilemmas in IT and business through debate.

Call us on About this subject What you'll learn On successful completion you will be able to: Evaluate diverse funding options for supporting entrepreneurial endeavors while considering the essential development steps. Critically analyse IT business ventures based on social, legal and ethical issues and examine the responsibilities of IT professionals to employers, clients, and society as part of a team. Reflect on personal viewpoints related to managing relationships and individual entrepreneurial capacity, effectively developing and presenting personal and professional reflections to become career-ready. Apply ethical theory to analyse ethical dilemmas in IT and business through debate. Topics covered Introduction to Entrepreneurship and the Role of Entrepreneurship and Problem Solving in Business Learn Model Canvas Cost structures, Revenue Streams, Financing Start Ups and Exit Strategies Ethics and I.T, Decision Making and Conflict Management Communication and Intercultural Communication, CVs and Interviews Description Entrepreneurship is an essential element of today’s society. This subject will outline the basic principles of Entrepreneurship. It will examine the steps required in developing an idea into a business and will explore the tools and necessary insights to make a successful venture. You will interpret and apply theory to case studies. Guest speakers will present and discuss start-up issues, pitfalls, and the ingredients for success. You will also develop professional skills related to ethical and moral decision making, and evaluate the social implications of entrepreneurship in the broader global context. Assessments Developing a business plan & delivering a pitch (equiv to 1500 words/student). Consists of two parts: 1. The pitch presentation due in Week 4 worth 20%, 2. Business plan worth 25% due in Week 6 (45%) Employment preparation task (equivalent to 1200 words) Resume and Cover letter, Interview questions. (25%) Scenario based ethics case study (equivalent to 1500 words) (30%)

courses.assessment: Subject may require attendance 5 July 2026 Entry requirements Prior study needed Start dates 13 July 2026 Learn more about Pricing Info Upfront cost Learn more about Upfront Cost Loan available HECS-HELP and FEE-HELP available Learn more about financial options Introduction to Computer Forensics Start studying Need expert guidance? Summarise ethical and legal considerations in the documentation of forensic evidence. Analyse digital evidence with physical forensic evidence tools and techniques. Apply  investigative procedures  to perform forensic Investigations of different types of digital systems. Topics covered Digital Forensic Tools and Data Acquisition Processing Crime Scene, Analysis and Validation Computer Basics for Digital Forensic (Data Storage, File System, Data Structure) Forensic Investigation of Operating System (Windows System) Recovering Graphics Email Forensics Digital Forensic for Mobile Devices Cloud Forensics and RAM Forensic Technical report, Expert witness Description This subject considers the procedures related to digital forensic investigation in computing and networking scenarios. Students critically appraise the key concepts associated with data hiding, evidence collection and validation and traffic analysis required to perform forensic analysis in a digital environment. Students learn the importance of legal considerations in the documentation of forensic evidence. Case studies are incorporated for students to apply practical forensic tools. Assessments Online quiz  (equiv to 750 words) designed to simulate a real-world digital forensic investigation. (25%) Technical report (equiv 1750 words) that simulates a real-world digital forensic investigation (35%) Closed book, supervised and centrally administered exam (equiv to 2000 words) (40%)

courses.name: Intermediate Network Engineering
courses.description: Enrolments for this course are closed, but you may have other options to start studying now. Book a consultation to learn more.
learning_outcomes: On successful completion you will be able to:
Describe and evaluate IPng addressing scheme and subnetting and their issues.
Implement distance vector and link state routing protocols.
Implement multiprotocol network.
Recognize the strengths and weaknesses of routing protocols.
Broadly explain the scope of the TCP and UDP protocols.

Implement distance vector and link state routing protocols. Implement multiprotocol network. Recognize the strengths and weaknesses of routing protocols. Broadly explain the scope of the TCP and UDP protocols. Topics covered IP addressing scheme and complex subnetting and their issues Distance vector routing protocols Link state routing protocols Network with multiple routing protocol External network routing protocol (BGP) Description In this subject, students will explore the structure and function of routers and examine key network routing concepts. The subject covers both static and dynamic routing, focusing on core protocols such as distance vector, link state and path vector. Students will gain hands-on experience in configuring routers, building and interpreting routing tables, and evaluating the performance of these protocols across diverse network environments. Assessments Consists of two components. First is a domain knowledge quiz 10% (equiv 500 words). Second is a problem solving test 20% (equiv 1000 words) (30%) Lab Practical Test (1000 word equiv), focuses on configuring, and verifying different nework routing protocols. (25%) Final Exam: Centrally Scheduled (2000 word equiv) Comprehensive online theory exam covering all modules and measures overall understanding of network infrastructure routing protocols operations. (45%)

Call us on About this subject What you'll learn On successful completion you will be able to: Review and compare different concepts of penetration testing Apply penetration testing methodologies and techniques. Identify and exploit common security vulnerabilities and recommend security controls. Develop and produce client reports, explaining key findings to diverse audiences. Topics covered Phases and Methodologies of Penetration Testing Reconnaissance and OSINT Vulnerability Assessment System Hacking Web Application Exploitation Penetration Testing of  Mobile Platform Social Engineering Wireless Penetration Testing Description Penetration testing encompasses the evaluation of organizational vulnerabilities by conducting thorough security testing. This subject provides students with a comprehensive understanding of the principles and procedures involved in system penetration testing and the ethical and legal obligations. It explores the utilization of popular software tools employed during penetration testing exercises. Through this course, students will acquire knowledge about different types of penetration testing and their respective phases, as well as gain expertise in interpreting results obtained from commonly utilized penetration testing tools. Additionally, students will recognize the significant value of penetration testing for businesses and organizations and will develop the skills to effectively report on and enhance an organization's security resilience using testing outcomes. Assessments Individual assessment (2000 word equiv) Students will perform a practical penetration testing exercise on a vulnerable system to identify open services, user accounts, vulnerabilities and hidden flags. (50%) Individual assessment (1500 word equiv) Students will prepare a technical report covering essential penetration testing tasks, including threat hunting, vulnerability assessment, exploitation and log analysis. (30%) Individual assessment (1000 words equiv) Students will complete an online quiz comprising practical tasks in Open-Source Intelligence (OSNIT) network reconnaissance and data discovery. (20%)

courses.name: Network Systems and Web Security
courses.description: Enrolments for this course are closed, but you may have other options to start studying now. Book a consultation to learn more.
learning_outcomes: On successful completion you will be able to:
Analyse systems and networks for vulnerabilities and security flaws, incorporating modern tools and techniques for comprehensive analysis.
Apply cryptographic algorithms, including encryption methods, hash functions, digital signatures, and key exchange protocols, to secure systems, networks and clouds.
Examine and apply key security tools and protocols, such as Kerberos, PGP, IPSec, SSL/TLS, and emerging frameworks.
Identify and investigate ethical and legal considerations relevant to information privacy and security issues.

Call us on About this subject What you'll learn On successful completion you will be able to: Analyse systems and networks for vulnerabilities and security flaws, incorporating modern tools and techniques for comprehensive analysis. Apply cryptographic algorithms, including encryption methods, hash functions, digital signatures, and key exchange protocols, to secure systems, networks and clouds. Examine and apply key security tools and protocols, such as Kerberos, PGP, IPSec, SSL/TLS, and emerging frameworks. Identify and investigate ethical and legal considerations relevant to information privacy and security issues. Topics covered Introduction to cybersecurity Network endpoint security Introduction to cryptography Symmetric cryptography Asymmetric cryptography Data integrity algorithms Mutual trust, public key infrastructure and X.509 Layer 5 Network Security: Kerberos Layers 4 & 5 Network Security: SSL/TLS, HTTPS, and SSH Layer 3 Network Security: IPSec protocol Cloud security Description In this subject you will explore current and emerging topics in Network Systems and Web Security. Beginning with an analysis of security attacks on system services and a discussion of both traditional and modern security mechanisms, the subject expands to cover security paradigms across various domains. The subject topics include system security, network security, web security, cloud security, and innovative approaches in cybersecurity. Assessments Lab Activity - weekly submissions from Week 3 to Week 12 (1000-word equivalent total). (15%) Reflection on initial labs (equivalent to 1500 words of work) (35%) One workbook (2000 word equivalent) (50%)
